vBRIEFInfo.author: agent:claude-opus-4.8
vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF: enlarge the ride-screen temperature and add cold (<=40F) and conspicuous freeze (<=37F) warnings

narratives.Overview: Builds on the weather badge (PR #18) that shows the current temperature and a 6-hour rain warning in the top-left of the ride screen. This scope makes the temperature far more prominent and adds cold-weather safety cues for riders, who are acutely affected by low temperatures and road-icing risk.
narratives.Problem: The current temperature is small and easy to miss, and there is no warning for cold or potentially-icy conditions — conditions that materially affect rider safety and grip.
narratives.Goals:
1. Make the temperature value on the ride screen roughly twice as large.
2. Show a cold-weather warning when the temperature is 40F or below.
3. Show a conspicuous freeze warning, with a snowflake icon, when the temperature is 37F or below.
4. Evaluate thresholds correctly regardless of whether the user displays Fahrenheit or Celsius.
narratives.FunctionalRequirements:
FR-1: The temperature value MUST render approximately 100% larger than its previous size while staying theme-colored and legible.
FR-2: When the current temperature is <= 40F (or the Celsius equivalent), a cold-weather warning indicator MUST appear with the temperature.
FR-3: When the current temperature is <= 37F (or the Celsius equivalent), a conspicuous freeze warning including a snowflake icon MUST appear and take visual priority over the cold indicator (a freezing reading is not also shown as merely 'cold').
FR-4: Warning thresholds MUST be evaluated against a canonical Fahrenheit value so they behave identically in F and C display modes.
narratives.Architecture: No new services. Add a pure TemperatureWarning derivation (none/cold/freezing) on WeatherSnapshot, computed from the stored temperature normalized to Fahrenheit and compared against named thresholds (cold 40F, freeze 37F). WeatherBadgeView is updated to (a) render the temperature at ~2x size and (b) render the cold indicator / conspicuous snowflake freeze warning based on the derived level. Thresholds live as constants on WeatherSnapshot so they are testable without UI.
narratives.TestingStrategy: Unit-test the pure TemperatureWarning logic across boundaries in both units: 41F->none, 40F->cold, 38F->cold, 37F->freezing, 30F->freezing, and Celsius equivalents (e.g. 10C->none, 3C->cold, 2C->freezing). The font-size and icon presentation are validated by build + manual/on-device review since they are not unit-testable.

narrative.Acceptance: The temperature value on the ride screen renders at roughly twice its previous size, stays theme-colored, and does not break the top-bar layout.
When the current temperature is 40F or below (or the Celsius equivalent), a cold indicator appears alongside the temperature.

items.title: Conspicuous freeze warning with snowflake at <= 37F

narrative.Acceptance: When the current temperature is 37F or below (or the Celsius equivalent), a conspicuous freeze warning with a snowflake icon appears and takes visual priority over the cold indicator.
Warning level is derived from a canonical Fahrenheit value; boundary behavior is unit-tested in both Fahrenheit and Celsius.
